keyPoints: No direct published A/B field test of explicit social step-back prompts in AI-enabled apps.
Design a three-arm randomized trial: Control, Step-Back Pause, Step-Back Pause + Real-Person Referral.
Primary outcomes: real-world social interactions and parasocial time; loneliness as a key secondary outcome.
Mediation and moderation: test whether effects operate via reduced AI usage, increased real-world actions, and whether baseline loneliness or attachment to AI moderates effects.
Rigorous measurement: objective in-app analytics plus validated loneliness and parasocial scales; privacy-preserving proxies for real-world activity.
Pre-register hypotheses, analysis plan, sample size, and data handling; include false discovery control.
Ground design in social-recognition theory and ethics, balancing AI support with real-world social opportunities and guardrails against parasocial harms.
